check: Multiple distinct creative concepts running per ad set not just color/copy variants
description: At least 3-5 genuinely different creative concepts (not palette swaps of one idea) live per audience/ad set, especially on platforms using entity-level retrieval (Meta Andromeda, TikTok)
why_it_matters: Algorithmic delivery systems increasingly retrieve by creative entity not just targeting; low concept diversity caps how much of the addressable audience the system can actually reach

check: Frequency and CTR-decay monitored as fatigue signals
description: Frequency trending upward alongside declining CTR/CVR on a specific creative tracked as an explicit fatigue signal not discovered only when ROAS drops
why_it_matters: Waiting for ROAS to visibly drop before refreshing creative means weeks of degraded spend efficiency already happened

check: Creative refresh cadence defined per spend tier
description: A concrete refresh cadence (e.g. new creative every 2-4 weeks for top-spend campaigns) rather than refreshing reactively whenever someone notices
why_it_matters: High-spend campaigns burn through audience saturation fastest and need the most proactive refresh discipline

check: Vertical/native-aspect video present not only static or horizontal assets
description: Vertical 9:16 or platform-native aspect creative present for any platform with a Stories/Reels/For-You-feed placement
why_it_matters: Static or horizontal-only creative in a vertical feed placement reads as low-effort and gets algorithmically and visually deprioritized

check: UGC-style or platform-native creative included in the mix
description: At least some creative styled to look native to the platform (not obviously produced/branded) alongside polished brand creative
why_it_matters: Platform-native-looking creative consistently outperforms overtly polished ads on TikTok/Reels/Stories placements

check: 70/20/10 budget split applied across proven scaling and test spend
description: Roughly 70% of budget on proven winning campaigns, 20% on campaigns showing early promise, 10% on new tests — not concentrated entirely on either extreme
why_it_matters: All-in on proven winners misses the next winner; all-in on tests wastes budget that should be scaling what already works

check: 3x Kill Rule applied to underperforming campaigns
description: A campaign spending 3x the target CPA/CAC with zero conversions is paused or restructured rather than left running on hope
why_it_matters: Undefined kill criteria is how budget quietly bleeds into campaigns that were never going to convert

check: Budget increases capped at roughly 20% per adjustment
description: Budget scaled up in increments of about 20% rather than large jumps that reset the learning phase or destabilize delivery
why_it_matters: Large budget jumps push campaigns back into an unstable learning phase and can spike CPA temporarily

check: Platform ad policy review completed before launch for regulated categories
description: Campaigns in regulated categories (health, finance, housing, employment) reviewed against platform-specific policy restrictions before launch not discovered via rejection
why_it_matters: Regulated-category violations can trigger account-level restrictions beyond just the individual ad being rejected

check: Required disclosures and disclaimers present per platform and region
description: Political financial or health-adjacent ads carry the specific disclosure format each platform and region requires
why_it_matters: Missing required disclosures is both a policy violation and in some categories a legal compliance gap independent of the platform
